about_text: Speedreeder shows one word at a time (RSVP) so you can read with less eye movement. Import .txt or .epub — everything stays on your device. A project by IntellectShop.
tips_text:
1. Start at a comfortable words-per-minute rate and increase gradually.

2. You can pause anytime — progress is saved per book.

3. Very large books may hit browser storage limits; consider splitting long texts.

4. Dark theme is easier on the eyes for long sessions.

5. The red letter is the “optimal recognition point” (ORP): your eye locks onto a consistent spot in each word, similar to Spritz.

6. Leading/trailing punctuation is stripped when splitting; apostrophes in don't and hyphens in well-known are kept.

7. On import you can set a “start phrase” — useful after converting PDF to plain text.

8. On a phone, double-tap the left or right screen edge to decrease or increase speed by 10 wpm; on desktop use the − and + buttons next to play.
feedback_email_invalid: That does not look like a valid email
feedback_success: Thank you — your message was sent.
feedback_error: Could not send. Please try again later.
feedback_error_http: The server returned HTTP {code}. Often the host must route /api-feedback to MODX (see site .htaccess / nginx).
empty_library:
No books yet.
Tap Import — .txt and .epub are supported; text stays on this device only.
snack_pdf_not_supported: PDF is not supported yet — convert to TXT or EPUB
import_start_help: Find a phrase or select a position directly in the text. Tap “Start here” — the book will be added starting from that spot.
import_start_truncated: Only the beginning is shown (very long text). If your spot is not here, use search or trim/split the file first.
import_anchor_help: To skip a foreword or boilerplate, type a short unique phrase from the book — reading starts at its first occurrence (the phrase is included). Leave empty to import the full text.
snack_anchor_not_found: Phrase not found in the text
storage_notice: Texts are stored only on this device.
